test_cases.test_case: Successful login with standard_user
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
Network connection available
steps: 1. Enter 'standard_user' in the Username field
2. Enter 'secret_sauce' in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: User is authenticated and redirected to the Product Inventory page; Inventory page elements (product list, header, and cart icon) are visible

test_cases.test_case: Successful login with problem_user
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
Network connection available
steps: 1. Enter 'problem_user' in the Username field
2. Enter 'secret_sauce' in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: User is authenticated and redirected to the Product Inventory page; page loads and product list is displayed (note: product images or behavior may be intentionally problematic but login succeeds)

test_cases.test_case: Successful login with visual_user
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
Network connection available
steps: 1. Enter 'visual_user' in the Username field
2. Enter 'secret_sauce' in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: User is authenticated and redirected to the Product Inventory page; login succeeds though visual differences may be present for this user

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with locked_out_user
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
steps: 1. Enter 'locked_out_user' in the Username field
2. Enter 'secret_sauce' in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner is displayed with the message 'Epic sadface: Sorry, this user has been locked out.'; the user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with invalid username and/or password
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
steps: 1. Enter 'invalid_user' in the Username field
2. Enter 'wrong_password' in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner is displayed with the message 'Epic sadface: Username and password do not match any user in this service.'; the user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with missing username
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field empty
2. Enter 'secret_sauce' in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner is displayed with the message 'Epic sadface: Username is required.'; the user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with missing password
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
steps: 1. Enter 'standard_user' in the Username field
2. Leave the Password field empty
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner is displayed with the message 'Epic sadface: Password is required.'; the user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to log in with both username and password empty
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
steps: 1. Leave the Username field empty
2. Leave the Password field empty
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner is displayed indicating required credentials (e.g., 'Epic sadface: Username is required.' or combined validation); the user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Attempt SQL injection or special characters in username and password fields
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
steps: 1. Enter "' OR '1'='1" in the Username field
2. Enter "' OR '1'='1" in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: Login is rejected; an error banner displays 'Epic sadface: Username and password do not match any user in this service.' (no unauthorized access granted)

test_cases.test_case: Login with performance_glitch_user (verify eventual success despite delay)
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
Network connection available
steps: 1. Enter 'performance_glitch_user' in the Username field
2. Enter 'secret_sauce' in the Password field
3. Click Login and observe response time
test_cases.expected_result: The application may respond slowly, but the user is eventually authenticated and redirected to the Product Inventory page; no functional failure occurs due to delay

test_cases.test_case: Login attempt using username with leading and trailing whitespace
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
steps: 1. Enter ' standard_user ' (with leading and trailing spaces) in the Username field
2. Enter 'secret_sauce' in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: If the system trims input, the user is authenticated and redirected to the Product Inventory page; if not, an error banner displays 'Epic sadface: Username and password do not match any user in this service.'; tester should document actual behavior

test_cases.test_case: Login attempt with a very long username (boundary test)
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page
steps: 1. Enter a very long username (e.g., 500 characters) in the Username field
2. Enter 'secret_sauce' in the Password field
3. Click Login
test_cases.expected_result: Login is rejected with an appropriate error (e.g., 'Epic sadface: Username and password do not match any user in this service.') or an input validation message; application does not crash

test_cases.test_case: Add a single product to the cart updates button and cart badge
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed
Cart is empty
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, locate a product (e.g., 'Sauce Labs Backpack')
2. Click the product's 'Add to cart' button
test_cases.expected_result: The product's button text changes to 'Remove'; the cart badge appears with count '1'; the product remains listed on the inventory page

test_cases.test_case: Remove a product from the cart updates button and cart badge
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed
At least one product has been added to the cart (cart badge shows >=1)
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, locate a product with button 'Remove'
2. Click the product's 'Remove' button
test_cases.expected_result: The product's button text changes back to 'Add to cart'; the cart badge decrements by 1 (or disappears if count becomes 0)

test_cases.test_case: Open Product Detail page by clicking product name
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, click the name/title of a product
2. Observe navigation to the Product Detail page
test_cases.expected_result: The Product Detail page opens for the selected product and displays the correct product name, description, and price

test_cases.test_case: Open Product Detail page by clicking product image
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, click the image of a product
2. Observe navigation to the Product Detail page
test_cases.expected_result: The Product Detail page opens for the selected product and displays the correct product name, description, and price

test_cases.test_case: Sort products by Name (A–Z) displays ascending alphabetical order
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed with multiple products
steps: 1. Open the sort dropdown on the Product Inventory page
2. Select 'Name (A to Z)' or equivalent option
3. Observe the order of product names in the list
test_cases.expected_result: Products are displayed in ascending alphabetical order by product name (A–Z); product entries still show name, description, price, and 'Add to cart' button

test_cases.test_case: Sort products by Name (Z–A) displays descending alphabetical order
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed with multiple products
steps: 1. Open the sort dropdown on the Product Inventory page
2. Select 'Name (Z to A)' or equivalent option
3. Observe the order of product names in the list
test_cases.expected_result: Products are displayed in descending alphabetical order by product name (Z–A)

test_cases.test_case: Sort products by Price (low–high and high–low) displays correct numeric order
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed with products having varying prices
steps: 1. Open the sort dropdown and select 'Price (low to high)'
2. Verify the products are ordered from lowest price to highest price
3. Open the sort dropdown and select 'Price (high to low)'
4. Verify the products are ordered from highest price to lowest price
test_cases.expected_result: Products reorder correctly for both price sort options; prices are displayed and numeric ordering is correct

test_cases.test_case: Add all available products to the cart and verify cart badge and button states
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed
Cart is empty
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, click 'Add to cart' for every product listed
2. Observe the cart badge after adding all items
3. Verify the state of each product's add/remove button
test_cases.expected_result: Cart badge count equals the total number of products listed; every product's button text is 'Remove'; inventory still displays product details

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggle Add to cart and Remove for a product and verify final state
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed
Cart is empty
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, pick a product and click 'Add to cart' then immediately click 'Remove', repeat multiple times rapidly
2. After the rapid toggles, inspect the product's button text and the cart badge count
test_cases.expected_result: After rapid toggling, the product button shows the final correct state ('Add to cart' if removed, 'Remove' if added) and the cart badge accurately reflects the final number of items (no negative counts or duplicate increments)

test_cases.test_case: Sort behavior when multiple products have identical names or identical prices
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed with at least two products sharing the same name or same price
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, apply 'Name (A to Z)' and observe items with identical names
2. Apply 'Price (low to high)' and observe items with identical prices
3. Note the relative ordering of items that have identical sort keys
test_cases.expected_result: Products with identical sort keys remain in a consistent order (stable sort) or follow the application's documented secondary sort rule; no products are lost or duplicated

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access Product Inventory page without being logged in
preconditions: User is logged out
Browser is at application base URL or direct inventory URL
steps: 1. Navigate directly to the Product Inventory page URL or refresh the page while logged out
2. Observe the resulting page or redirection
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Login page (or shown an authentication required message) and cannot see the Product Inventory; no 'Add to cart' buttons or cart badge are accessible

test_cases.test_case: Select an invalid sort option (manipulated/unsupported) and verify system handles it gracefully
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product Inventory page is displayed
steps: 1. Using developer tools or an automated script, set the sort dropdown value to an unsupported/invalid option (e.g., 'unknown') and trigger the change event
2. Observe the product list and any error messages
test_cases.expected_result: The application does not crash or expose raw errors; it either ignores the invalid sort and retains the previous/default order or falls back to a documented default sort; no visible error stack traces are shown to the user

test_cases.test_case: Open Product Detail and verify UI elements
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
From Product Inventory, at least one product is visible
steps: 1. From the Product Inventory page, click a product name or image to open its Product Detail page
2. Observe the product image, name, description, and price
3. Observe the Add to cart / Remove button state
4. Observe the Back to products button and the cart icon in the header
test_cases.expected_result: Product image, name, description, and price are displayed; Add to cart (or Remove if already in cart) button is present; Back to products button and cart icon are visible and enabled

test_cases.test_case: Add product to cart from Product Detail
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Selected product is not in the cart
User is on that product's Product Detail page
steps: 1. On the Product Detail page click the 'Add to cart' button
2. Observe the button text/state after the click
3. Observe the cart badge in the header
test_cases.expected_result: Button text changes from 'Add to cart' to 'Remove'; cart badge increments by 1 and reflects the new cart count; product can be seen in the Shopping Cart page

test_cases.test_case: Remove product from cart from Product Detail
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Selected product is already in the cart
User is on that product's Product Detail page
steps: 1. On the Product Detail page click the 'Remove' button
2. Observe the button text/state after the click
3. Observe the cart badge in the header
test_cases.expected_result: Button text changes from 'Remove' to 'Add to cart'; cart badge decrements by 1 (or disappears if count becomes 0); product is removed from the Shopping Cart page

test_cases.test_case: Navigate back to Product Inventory and to Shopping Cart from Product Detail
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
User is on any Product Detail page
steps: 1. Click 'Back to products' on the Product Detail page
2. From Product Inventory, click the cart icon in the header
test_cases.expected_result: Clicking 'Back to products' returns user to the Product Inventory page; clicking the cart icon navigates to the Shopping Cart page

test_cases.test_case: Open Product Detail with an invalid/non-existent product ID in the URL
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user or on login page
User can edit the browser URL
steps: 1. Manually modify the browser URL to reference a non-existent product id (e.g. /inventory-item.html?id=9999) and press Enter
2. Observe the behavior of the application
test_cases.expected_result: Application shows an error message or redirects to the Product Inventory page; Product Detail content is not shown for the invalid id and no broken/empty UI is presented

test_cases.test_case: Product Detail fails to load due to server/network error
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
User is on Product Inventory page
steps: 1. Simulate a server or network error for the product detail API (e.g. block request or return 500)
2. Click a product to open its Product Detail page
3. Observe any error UI presented
test_cases.expected_result: An informative error message is displayed (e.g. 'Failed to load product details'); user remains on a recoverable state (option to retry or navigate back to products) and the UI does not crash

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to add to cart while offline
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
User is on a Product Detail page
Browser can be set to offline mode
steps: 1. Turn the browser/network to offline mode
2. Click the 'Add to cart' button on the Product Detail page
3. Observe the result and any user-facing error or retry option
test_cases.expected_result: Operation fails gracefully with an error or offline indicator; the UI displays a message such as 'Unable to update cart while offline' and the local button state does not incorrectly indicate success

test_cases.test_case: Open Product Detail for a product already in the cart and verify initial state
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Product A has already been added to the cart from another page
steps: 1. From Product Inventory click to open Product A's Product Detail page
2. Observe the Add to cart / Remove button and the cart badge
test_cases.expected_result: Product Detail shows the button as 'Remove' (reflecting that the item is in the cart); cart badge count matches the number of items in the cart

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly toggle Add to cart and Remove to check UI and cart count consistency
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
User is on a Product Detail page
Network is responsive
steps: 1. Repeatedly click 'Add to cart' then 'Remove' on the Product Detail page several times in quick succession (e.g. 5-10 toggles)
2. After toggling, navigate to the Shopping Cart and refresh Product Detail page
3. Observe the final button state and cart badge/count
test_cases.expected_result: Final button state and cart badge accurately reflect the true cart contents (no duplicate entries or stale counts); application remains stable with no UI glitches

test_cases.test_case: Display product with extremely long name and description
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Test product exists with an extremely long name and description (very long strings)
steps: 1. From Product Inventory click the test product with long text to open Product Detail
2. Observe layout, wrapping, scroll behavior, and visibility of Add to cart and Back to products controls
test_cases.expected_result: Long name and description wrap or scroll without breaking layout; Add to cart, Back to products and cart icon remain visible and usable; no overflow causes controls to become inaccessible

test_cases.test_case: Remove a single item from the cart
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product has been added to the cart
steps: 1. Click the shopping cart icon to navigate to the Shopping Cart page
2. Locate an item listed in the cart and click its 'Remove' button
test_cases.expected_result: The selected item is removed from the Shopping Cart list; the cart badge in the header decreases by one (or disappears if no items remain); the removed item's entry (description and quantity) is no longer visible

test_cases.test_case: Return to Product Inventory using Continue Shopping
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart contains one or more items
steps: 1. From the Shopping Cart page, click the 'Continue Shopping' button
2. Observe the destination page
test_cases.expected_result: The application navigates to the Product Inventory page (header/title shows 'Products'); the cart badge remains visible in the header reflecting current cart count

test_cases.test_case: Begin checkout from Shopping Cart
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart contains one or more items
steps: 1. From the Shopping Cart page, click the 'Checkout' button
2. Observe the next page
test_cases.expected_result: The user is navigated to the Checkout: Your Information page (checkout flow begins) and the cart contents are shown on the following overview step

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to start checkout with an empty cart
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart is empty
steps: 1. Navigate to the Shopping Cart page
2. Click the 'Checkout' button
test_cases.expected_result: Checkout is not completed: either the Checkout button is disabled or the application remains on the Shopping Cart page and displays an appropriate message (for example, 'Your cart is empty') indicating checkout cannot proceed

test_cases.test_case: Access the Shopping Cart page without being logged in
preconditions: User is logged out
steps: 1. Attempt to navigate directly to the Shopping Cart page URL or click a cart link while logged out
test_cases.expected_result: The application redirects to the Login page (or shows a login prompt) and does not display cart items; user must authenticate before accessing the Shopping Cart

test_cases.test_case: Add all available products, verify each cart entry shows quantity 1 and has 'Remove' button
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart is empty
Multiple products available in inventory
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, click 'Add to cart' for every product listed
2. Click the shopping cart icon to open the Shopping Cart page
3. Inspect each item row for quantity and the presence of a 'Remove' button
4. Observe the cart badge count in the header
test_cases.expected_result: Every product added appears in the Shopping Cart as a separate line item with quantity displayed as '1' and a 'Remove' button; the cart badge count equals the number of products added; all 'Add to cart' buttons on the inventory have changed to 'Remove'

test_cases.test_case: Remove the last item and verify empty cart state and badge disappearance
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart contains exactly one item
steps: 1. Navigate to the Shopping Cart page
2. Click 'Remove' for the only item in the cart
3. Observe the Shopping Cart page and header cart badge
test_cases.expected_result: The cart list shows no items (empty state); the cart badge in the header disappears or is not shown (count zero); UI does not display leftover item entries or 'Remove' buttons

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly remove multiple items to verify UI consistency and count synchronization
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart contains multiple items
steps: 1. Open the Shopping Cart page
2. Rapidly click 'Remove' on several different items in quick succession (one after another)
3. Observe the cart list and cart badge after each removal
test_cases.expected_result: UI remains responsive; each removed item disappears from the list; the cart badge count updates accurately after each removal without temporary duplication or negative counts; no JavaScript errors are shown to the user

test_cases.test_case: Enter valid First Name, Last Name, Postal Code and continue to Overview
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product has been added to the cart
User is on the Checkout: Your Information page
steps: 1. In the First Name field enter 'John'
2. In the Last Name field enter 'Doe'
3. In the Postal Code field enter '90210'
4. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: User is navigated to the Checkout: Overview page; order summary and item list are displayed; no error banner is shown

test_cases.test_case: Click Cancel returns user to the Shopping Cart
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product has been added to the cart
User is on the Checkout: Your Information page
steps: 1. Click Cancel on the Checkout: Your Information page
test_cases.expected_result: User is returned to the Shopping Cart page; cart contents remain unchanged; no Checkout error banner is shown

test_cases.test_case: Accept names with hyphen/apostrophe and postal code with hyphen
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product in cart
User is on the Checkout: Your Information page
steps: 1. In the First Name field enter 'Anne-Marie'
2. In the Last Name field enter "O'Connor"
3. In the Postal Code field enter '12345-6789'
4. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: User is navigated to the Checkout: Overview page; entered name and postal code are accepted and displayed on the Overview summary; no error banner is shown

test_cases.test_case: Submit form with First Name missing
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product in cart
User is on the Checkout: Your Information page
steps: 1. Leave First Name empty
2. In the Last Name field enter 'Doe'
3. In the Postal Code field enter '90210'
4. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner is displayed with the message 'Error: First Name is required'; the user remains on the Checkout: Your Information page

test_cases.test_case: Submit form with Last Name missing
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product in cart
User is on the Checkout: Your Information page
steps: 1. In the First Name field enter 'John'
2. Leave Last Name empty
3. In the Postal Code field enter '90210'
4. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner is displayed with the message 'Error: Last Name is required'; the user remains on the Checkout: Your Information page

test_cases.test_case: Submit form with Postal Code missing
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product in cart
User is on the Checkout: Your Information page
steps: 1. In the First Name field enter 'John'
2. In the Last Name field enter 'Doe'
3. Leave Postal Code empty
4. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner is displayed with the message 'Error: Postal Code is required'; the user remains on the Checkout: Your Information page

test_cases.test_case: Enter very long values in all fields (boundary length)
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product in cart
User is on the Checkout: Your Information page
steps: 1. In the First Name field enter a 256-character string (e.g. 'A' repeated 256 times)
2. In the Last Name field enter a 256-character string
3. In the Postal Code field enter a 50-character string
4. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: The application accepts or reliably handles the long inputs without crashing; if accepted, user is navigated to the Checkout: Overview page and the entered values are preserved or appropriately truncated with no unexpected errors

test_cases.test_case: Enter only whitespace characters in fields
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product in cart
User is on the Checkout: Your Information page
steps: 1. In the First Name field enter '   ' (spaces)
2. In the Last Name field enter '   ' (spaces)
3. In the Postal Code field enter '   ' (spaces)
4. Click Continue
test_cases.expected_result: Whitespace-only input is treated as missing input; an error banner is displayed (at minimum 'Error: First Name is required') and the user remains on the Checkout: Your Information page

test_cases.test_case: Complete order from Overview by clicking Finish
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product is in the cart
User has completed Checkout: Information step (first name, last name, zip provided)
User is on the Checkout: Overview page
steps: 1. Review the list of items and totals shown on the Overview page
2. Confirm payment and shipping information displayed are correct
3. Click the Finish button
test_cases.expected_result: The app navigates to the confirmation page showing 'Thank you for your order!'; the shopping cart badge is cleared (no items shown); order summary/receipt is displayed on the confirmation page

test_cases.test_case: Exit checkout from Overview using Cancel
preconditions: User logged in
One or more products in the cart
User is on the Checkout: Overview page
steps: 1. Click the Cancel button on the Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: The app exits the checkout flow and navigates back to the Shopping Cart page; all items remain in the cart and quantities are unchanged

test_cases.test_case: Finish fails when server returns an order submission error
preconditions: User logged in
Cart contains one or more items
User is on the Checkout: Overview page
Order submission API is mocked or forced to return an error (e.g., 500 response)
steps: 1. Click the Finish button on the Overview page while the backend is returning an error
test_cases.expected_result: An error message/banner is displayed indicating the order could not be completed (e.g., 'Order submission failed. Please try again.'); the user remains on the Overview page and cart contents are not cleared

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to complete checkout from Overview with an empty cart
preconditions: User logged in
Cart is empty (user navigated to Overview URL or manipulated flow to reach Overview with empty cart)
User is on the Checkout: Overview page
steps: 1. Confirm the Items list on the Overview page shows no products
2. Click the Finish button
test_cases.expected_result: The system prevents order completion, displays an error message such as 'Your cart is empty' or redirects user back to the Shopping Cart page; no confirmation page is shown and no order is created

test_cases.test_case: Add all available products to cart and verify totals and cart badge on Overview
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart is empty
steps: 1. On the Product Inventory page, click 'Add to cart' for every product available
2. Click the cart icon and proceed through Checkout: Information step with valid data
3. On the Checkout: Overview page, note the item total, tax, and total
4. Verify the cart badge count in the header equals the number of products added
test_cases.expected_result: Cart badge equals the total number of products added; Overview page lists every product added; Item total equals the sum of each product price; Tax is calculated correctly from Item total and Total equals Item total plus Tax; all product action buttons in inventory changed to 'Remove' previously

test_cases.test_case: Display long payment and shipping information on Overview (text wrapping/truncation)
preconditions: User logged in
At least one product in cart
User completes Checkout: Information step using very long strings for first name, last name, and postal code fields (extremely long names/addresses)
steps: 1. Proceed to Checkout: Overview page after submitting long payment and shipping information
2. Observe how the payment and shipping information is rendered on the Overview page
test_cases.expected_result: Long payment and shipping fields are displayed without breaking layout (text either wraps or is truncated with ellipsis according to UI spec); no overlapping UI elements or horizontal scrolling is introduced and the Finish/Cancel buttons remain visible and usable

test_cases.test_case: Verify totals and rounding when item prices produce fractional tax amounts
preconditions: User logged in
Cart contains items with prices that when summed produce tax requiring rounding (e.g., prices that lead to fractional cents)
steps: 1. Proceed through Checkout to the Overview page
2. Note the Item total, Tax amount, and Total amount
3. Calculate expected Tax and Total using the configured tax rate and rounding rules and compare to displayed values
test_cases.expected_result: Displayed Tax and Total match the expected values according to the application's rounding rules (no off-by-one-cent errors); Item total equals sum of item prices

test_cases.test_case: Display success message and Back Home clears cart after completing checkout
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one product in the cart
User has completed Checkout Information and is on the Overview page
steps: 1. On the Overview page, click Finish to complete the order
2. Observe the Confirmation page
3. Click the Back Home button
test_cases.expected_result: Confirmation page displays success message such as 'Thank you for your order!'; clicking Back Home navigates to the Product Inventory page and the cart is cleared (no cart badge visible or count = 0)

test_cases.test_case: Back Home clears cart when multiple items were purchased
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Multiple products (2 or more) were added to the cart and checkout completed to reach Confirmation page
steps: 1. On the Confirmation page, verify the presence of the success message
2. Click Back Home
3. On the Product Inventory page, verify the cart badge and cart content
test_cases.expected_result: User is navigated to Product Inventory; the cart badge is no longer visible (or shows 0) and the cart contains no items

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access Confirmation page directly without completing checkout
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart may be empty or have items, but user has NOT completed the checkout flow (has not clicked Finish)
steps: 1. Manually navigate to the Confirmation page URL (e.g., /checkout-complete.html) or click a direct link to it
2. Observe the resulting page
test_cases.expected_result: User is not shown the order confirmation for a completed purchase; the app either redirects to the Product Inventory or Checkout page (or shows an appropriate message indicating the order was not completed) and no 'Thank you for your order!' confirmation is displayed

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access Confirmation page while not logged in
preconditions: User is logged out
User attempts to access the Confirmation page URL directly
steps: 1. In a browser session where the user is not authenticated, navigate to the Confirmation page URL
2. Observe the resulting page or redirection
test_cases.expected_result: The application redirects the user to the Login page (or displays an authentication-required message); the Confirmation page and its 'Thank you for your order!' message are not displayed to unauthenticated users

test_cases.test_case: Refresh the Confirmation page repeatedly and verify Back Home remains functional
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
User has completed checkout and is on the Confirmation page
steps: 1. On the Confirmation page, verify the success message is displayed
2. Refresh the browser page several times (e.g., 3 times), observing the message each time
3. After the refreshes, click Back Home
test_cases.expected_result: The success message persists across refreshes; clicking Back Home after refresh navigates to Product Inventory and the cart is cleared (no cart badge visible)

test_cases.test_case: Click Back Home, then use browser Back to confirm cart remains cleared
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
User completed checkout and is on the Confirmation page
steps: 1. On the Confirmation page, click Back Home to return to Product Inventory
2. Verify the cart is cleared (no cart badge)
3. Use the browser Back button to return to the Confirmation page
4. Use the browser Forward button (or click Back Home again) to return to Product Inventory
test_cases.expected_result: After the initial Back Home click the cart is cleared; navigating back to the Confirmation page and forward again does not repopulate the cart — the cart remains empty on Product Inventory

test_cases.test_case: Click Back Home multiple times (idempotence) and verify stable behavior
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
User is on the Confirmation page after completing checkout
steps: 1. Click Back Home once and verify navigation to Product Inventory and that the cart is cleared
2. On Product Inventory, click the Back Home button again if present in header or repeat navigation action multiple times
3. Observe application behavior
test_cases.expected_result: Multiple Back Home clicks (or repeated navigation to home) do not cause errors; the user remains on Product Inventory and the cart remains cleared (no items added back)

test_cases.test_case: Logout from the Inventory page via the menu
preconditions: User is logged in (e.g., standard_user)
User is on the Product Inventory page
steps: 1. Click the hamburger/menu button in the header
2. Click 'Logout' in the menu
test_cases.expected_result: User is returned to the Login page (login form visible); protected pages (inventory, product detail, cart, checkout) are not accessible without logging in again

test_cases.test_case: Logout from a Product Detail page
preconditions: User is logged in
User is viewing a product detail page
steps: 1. On the product detail page, click the hamburger/menu button
2. Click 'Logout' in the menu
test_cases.expected_result: Application navigates to the Login page; attempting to access the product detail URL now redirects to the Login page until user logs in

test_cases.test_case: Logout while on the Cart or Checkout pages
preconditions: User is logged in
User is on the Shopping Cart page or Checkout Information page
steps: 1. Click the hamburger/menu button
2. Click 'Logout'
test_cases.expected_result: User is taken to the Login page; subsequent navigation to cart or checkout pages redirects to Login until authentication occurs

test_cases.test_case: Attempt to access Inventory URL directly after logging out
preconditions: User has logged out and is on the Login page
steps: 1. In the browser address bar, navigate directly to the inventory URL (e.g., /inventory.html)
2. Press Enter to load the page
test_cases.expected_result: User is redirected to the Login page (no access to inventory); protected content is not displayed without valid authentication

test_cases.test_case: Use browser Back button after logout to try to return to protected page
preconditions: User was on a protected page (inventory, detail, cart or checkout) and has just clicked Logout
steps: 1. After logout completes and the Login page is shown, click the browser Back button
2. Observe the page displayed
test_cases.expected_result: Protected content is not re-displayed; the application stays on the Login page or redirects back to Login if a protected URL is requested

test_cases.test_case: Attempt an authenticated action (e.g., add-to-cart API) using a stale session token after logout
preconditions: User was logged in, then performed Logout
Tester has captured the previous session cookie or token
steps: 1. Using a REST client or browser devtools, send an API request that requires authentication (for example POST /cart/add) including the old session cookie/token
2. Observe the response
test_cases.expected_result: Server rejects the request (401/302 to login or equivalent); the action is not performed and protected APIs require a valid, current authentication

test_cases.test_case: Logout in one browser tab, then interact with the app in a second tab that still shows protected content
preconditions: User is logged in in two browser tabs
Tab A is on Inventory, Tab B is also on Inventory or Product Detail
steps: 1. In Tab A, open the menu and click 'Logout' to log out
2. Switch to Tab B (which still displays protected content) and attempt an authenticated action (e.g., click 'Add to cart' or navigate to another protected page)
3. Observe the behavior in Tab B
test_cases.expected_result: Tab B does not allow the authenticated action; it either redirects to the Login page or prompts re-authentication. Protected actions require the user to log in again.

test_cases.test_case: Rapidly click Logout multiple times (double/triple click) to verify idempotence
preconditions: User is logged in
User is on any protected page featuring the menu logout option
steps: 1. Open the menu and click 'Logout' rapidly multiple times (double/triple click)
2. Observe the application's response and any errors
test_cases.expected_result: Application remains stable and ends the session once; user lands on Login page with no error dialogs or unexpected behaviors; repeated clicks do not produce duplicate sessions or errors

test_cases.test_case: Reset app state clears cart and resets Add/Remove buttons
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least two products have been added to the cart
User is on the Products (Inventory) page
steps: 1. Click the menu (burger) icon to open the side menu
2. Click 'Reset App State'
3. Close the menu (if it does not auto-close) and observe the header and product list
test_cases.expected_result: Cart badge is no longer visible (or shows 0); all product 'Remove' buttons that were previously shown revert to 'Add to cart'; cart contents are cleared; the user remains logged in (still on the Inventory page)

test_cases.test_case: Reset app state does not log the user out
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart contains one or more items
steps: 1. Note a visible indication that the user is logged in (e.g., menu accessible, username present if applicable)
2. Open the menu and click 'Reset App State'
3. After reset completes, attempt to access a user-only area (e.g., open Products page or open menu options)
test_cases.expected_result: User remains logged in and can access user-only pages; login page is not shown and session persists after reset

test_cases.test_case: Reset app state while on the Checkout flow clears the cart
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
At least one item in cart
User has navigated to Checkout: Information or Overview page
steps: 1. On the Checkout Information or Overview page, click the menu (burger) icon
2. Click 'Reset App State'
3. Return to the header and observe the cart badge and checkout page contents
test_cases.expected_result: Cart badge is removed or shows 0; checkout page reflects an empty cart (no items listed or item totals updated to zero); user remains logged in (not redirected to login)

test_cases.test_case: Reset App State option is not available when user is not logged in
preconditions: User is on the Swag Labs login page (not authenticated)
steps: 1. Attempt to open the application menu (if accessible) from the login page or check the UI for 'Reset App State' option
2. If the menu is not present, attempt to navigate to Inventory without logging in and inspect the menu
3. Try to perform any action that would trigger 'Reset App State'
test_cases.expected_result: 'Reset App State' option is not visible or accessible when the user is not authenticated; no state reset occurs and the user remains on the login page

test_cases.test_case: Reset App State fails with network/server error and shows an error message
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart contains one or more items
Test environment can simulate a server or network failure
steps: 1. Simulate a network/server error condition (e.g., disable network or mock server to return error for reset endpoint)
2. Open the menu and click 'Reset App State'
3. Observe any error message or banner and the cart state
test_cases.expected_result: An error banner or message is displayed indicating the reset failed (e.g., 'Could not reset app state'); the cart contents and add/remove button states remain unchanged until the operation succeeds; user remains logged in

test_cases.test_case: Reset App State when cart is already empty
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart is empty (no cart badge displayed)
steps: 1. Open the menu and click 'Reset App State'
2. Observe the header, product buttons, and any messages after the action
test_cases.expected_result: No errors occur; UI remains stable; cart remains empty and all product buttons show 'Add to cart'; user remains logged in

test_cases.test_case: Reset App State after adding all products (max items) to the cart
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
All available products have been added to the cart so cart badge equals total product count
steps: 1. Verify every product shows 'Remove' and cart badge equals total number of products
2. Open the menu and click 'Reset App State'
3. Verify each product button and the cart badge
test_cases.expected_result: Cart badge is removed or shows 0; every product button reverts to 'Add to cart'; cart contents cleared; user remains logged in

test_cases.test_case: Quick add then reset to test race condition
preconditions: User logged in as standard_user
Cart empty
steps: 1. Rapidly click 'Add to cart' on a product
2. Immediately open the menu and click 'Reset App State' before any delayed UI updates complete
3. Observe the final cart badge and button state after UI stabilizes
test_cases.expected_result: After UI stabilizes, cart is cleared and the product button shows 'Add to cart'; no duplicate or ghost items remain in the cart; user remains logged in

test_cases.test_case: Reset App State for performance_glitch_user (slow UI) clears state eventually
preconditions: User logged in as performance_glitch_user
At least one product in cart
Application may respond slowly
steps: 1. Open the menu and click 'Reset App State'
2. Wait an extended period (accounting for slow responses) and observe the cart badge and product buttons
3. Verify user is still logged in
test_cases.expected_result: Despite slow UI performance, after the operation completes the cart badge is removed or shows 0 and product buttons revert to 'Add to cart'; user remains logged in; no partial state remains

test_cases.test_case: Reset App State for problem_user (UI inconsistencies) resets visible state
preconditions: User logged in as problem_user
Cart contains items; product buttons may behave inconsistently for this user type
steps: 1. Open the menu and click 'Reset App State'
2. Observe product button labels, cart badge, and any visually inconsistent elements after reset
3. Manually verify cart contents via the Shopping Cart page
test_cases.expected_result: Cart is cleared and visible state is reset (cart badge removed, products show 'Add to cart'); any UI inconsistencies do not prevent the cart from being emptied; user remains logged in
